message:
feat(frankenterm): add frankenterm-core crate skeleton (bd-lff4p.1.2)

New host-agnostic VT/ANSI terminal engine crate with:
- Cell type with SGR attributes, colors (default/named/indexed/RGB)
- Grid (2D cell matrix) with scroll-up/down operations
- Cursor with position, visibility, pending-wrap, saved state (DECSC/DECRC)
- VT/ANSI parser skeleton (ground/esc/csi/osc states)
- Modes (origin, autowrap, insert, application cursor keys)
- Patch format for incremental grid updates
- Scrollback buffer with capacity-bounded ring eviction
- 27 tests, all passing; #![forbid(unsafe_code)]; clippy clean

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1320,7 +1320,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm VT/ANSI support matrix + conformance fixtures","description":"Goal
- Define exactly what FrankenTerm will implement (and what it will intentionally NOT implement) for VT/ANSI output streams.

Deliverables
- Support matrix for: C0/C1 controls, CSI sequences, SGR, OSC (incl OSC 8), DEC private modes, cursor modes, scrolling, insert/delete, tabs.
- A conformance fixture corpus (byte streams + expected grid snapshots) with deterministic replay.
- Clear "compat" boundaries: which behaviors must match de-facto terminals.

Acceptance Criteria
- Parser work can be driven directly from this matrix and fixtures.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449662450Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:39:49.449643043Z","close_reason":"Wrote comprehensive VT/ANSI support matrix at docs/spec/frankenterm-vt-support-matrix.md covering: C0/C1 controls, CSI (cursor, erase, insert/delete, scroll, modes), SGR (attributes, 16/256/RGB colors), OSC (title, hyperlinks, clipboard), DEC private modes, Kitty keyboard protocol, character sets. Includes 15 sections, explicit priority levels (Must/Should/Won't), parser state machine spec, conformance fixture format with 6 initial fixtures, and CI integration plan.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:27.823474788Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.10","title":"Bench: parser throughput + patch apply cost (core engine)","description":"Goal
- Build repeatable benchmarks for the core engine so optimization work is rigorous and gated.

Bench Corpus
- Maintain a realistic byte-stream corpus (build logs, dense SGR, markdown-ish output, unicode-heavy lines).
- Every benchmark run records corpus id/hash + git_sha so results are comparable.

Metrics
- Parser throughput (MB/s) for each corpus stream.
- Patch generation + patch apply cost under typical workloads.
- Memory footprint per 1k lines scrollback and under resize storms.

Outputs
- Machine-readable results (JSON) suitable for CI regression gates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Variance is characterized and documented; thresholds are set for meaningful regressions (p95 or distributional tests).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.11","title":"Fuzz: frankenterm-core parser and grid mutation entrypoints","description":"Goal
- Add fuzz targets to harden the parser and mutation logic.

Acceptance Criteria
- Fuzz harnesses run locally and in CI (time-bounded) with crash artifacts captured.
- Any found crashes become regression fixtures in the conformance corpus.

Wired into workspace Cargo.toml. Verified: cargo check -p frankenterm-core, cargo test -p frankenterm-core.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CyanTower","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512397161Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:49:05.512374489Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:50:32.893073810Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.3","title":"Implement: terminal grid data model (cells, lines, cursor, modes)","description":"Goal
- Implement the core in-memory model used by the terminal engine.

Requirements
- Cell model: text (grapheme), fg/bg, attrs (bold/italic/underline/etc), hyperlink id, and width.
- Grid model: fixed columns, rows; supports efficient row clears and spans.
- Cursor + modes: wrap, origin, insert, autowrap; plus placeholders for DEC modes.

Acceptance Criteria
- Unit tests for all basic mutations.
- No panics on edge cases (min size, zero-length operations).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.4","title":"Implement: scrollback + resize/reflow policy","description":"Goal
- Support realistic scrollback and correct behavior under resize.

Requirements
- Scrollback buffer with configurable capacity.
- Clear reflow policy: what happens to wrapped lines on resize.
- Resize invariants: cursor position, viewport, selection (if any), and mode semantics.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic replay fixtures for resize storms.
- Bench coverage for resize cost and memory.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.5","title":"Implement: selection model + copy extraction","description":"Goal
- Implement selection primitives (char/cell/word/line) and copy extraction from grid+scrollback.

Requirements
- Coordinate model for selections spanning scrollback and viewport.
- Word selection heuristics tuned for code and paths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests for selection across wrapped lines and resize.

@@ -1338,7 +1338,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","title":"Spec: TERM/capability profile + terminfo for FrankenTerm remote sessions","description":"Goal
- Ensure remote PTY apps behave correctly by defining FrankenTerm's terminal identity and capabilities.

Scope
- Decide what TERM will be set to in the PTY (e.g. `frankenterm`, `xterm-256color`, or a compatibility alias).
- If `TERM=frankenterm`, define and ship a terminfo entry (and a versioning story).
- Map terminfo capabilities to the VT/ANSI support matrix and reply engine behavior.

Acceptance Criteria
- A pinned capability profile exists (colors, mouse, paste, focus, kitty, hyperlinks) with explicit "must" vs "best-effort" behaviors.
- E2E remote scripts validate that common CLI apps render correctly under the chosen TERM profile.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.7","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:30.919814699Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","title":"Alien Artifact: queueing-theoretic backpressure + fairness policy (remote protocol)","description":"Goal
- Design backpressure/flow-control for the websocket protocol so we never OOM and we maintain low interactive latency under load.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Queueing model for input/output queues (arrival/service rates, tail latency).
- Explicit objective: minimize expected loss with an asymmetric loss matrix:
  - huge penalty for OOM/unbounded memory
  - large penalty for keystroke latency spikes
  - smaller penalty for throughput loss
- Use an evidence ledger: record measured rates, chosen thresholds, and why.

Deliverables
- A spec for bounded queues + drop/merge policy (e.g. coalesce resize/mouse-move events, chunk outputs).
- A fairness policy so long outputs don't starve input (interactive priority).

Acceptance Criteria
- Stress harness demonstrates bounded memory and stable p95 keystroke latency under worst-case output floods.
- Policy is explainable: given metrics, we can show why a drop/coalesce happened.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.8","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:42.047548858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2","title":"FrankenTerm Web: WebGPU renderer + web input plumbing","description":"Scope
- Build `frankenterm-web`: a WebGPU-first terminal renderer + input stack compiled to WASM (no xterm.js).

User-Facing Requirements
- Crisp text + stable geometry under resize/zoom/DPR changes.
- Smooth scrollback (virtualized rendering; no O(n) over history per frame).
- Selection + copy/paste, OSC 8 hyperlinks (hover/click), and in-terminal search.
- Accessibility strategy despite canvas/WebGPU rendering (DOM mirror mode, focus, high-contrast, reduced motion).

Deliverables
- `frankenterm-web` crate exporting a minimal JS/WASM API: init, resize, input, render/present.
- WebGPU renderer (glyph atlas + instancing) with deterministic patch application.
- Input capture: keyboard (modifiers/repeat), mouse + wheel, touch; clipboard; IME/composition.
- Bench + E2E harnesses emitting detailed JSONL for correctness + perf gates.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:03.657124887Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:17.880523076Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","title":"ADR: FrankenTerm WebGPU renderer architecture (glyph atlas, batching, present)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the renderer design for frankenterm-web.

Must Cover
- Rendering primitive set: background rects, text glyph quads, underline/strikethrough, cursor, selection.
- Glyph pipeline: bitmap vs SDF/MSDF; atlas packing; eviction; metrics.
- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.
- Color management: sRGB, blending rules for selections/cursor.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","title":"ADR: FrankenTerm WebGPU renderer architecture (glyph atlas, batching, present)","description":"Goal
- Decide and document the renderer design for frankenterm-web.

Must Cover
- Rendering primitive set: background rects, text glyph quads, underline/strikethrough, cursor, selection.
- Glyph pipeline: bitmap vs SDF/MSDF; atlas packing; eviction; metrics.
- Batching: instancing strategy, buffer update strategy (full vs dirty spans), CPU->GPU transfer budgets.
- Color management: sRGB, blending rules for selections/cursor.

Acceptance Criteria
- Renderer tasks can be implemented without ambiguity.
- Includes a measurable frame budget and how we will profile it.
","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:47:59.999763960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:51:40.955523135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","title":"Bench: web renderer frame-time harness + regression thresholds","description":"Goal
- Build a repeatable web performance harness for FrankenTerm that supports the Extreme Optimization loop.

Metrics (minimum)
- Frame time histogram (p50/p95/p99) at 80x24 and 120x40.
- Time-to-first-frame and time-to-interactive.
- Patch statistics: bytes per frame, dirty-span counts, draw-call counts.
- Memory usage (JS + WASM) steady-state and after stress (scrollback/resize).
- GPU timings when available (best-effort).

Outputs
- Machine-readable JSON for CI gating and local profiling.
- Optional JSONL stream for per-frame/per-event diagnostics (run_id, git_sha, browser, DPR).

Acceptance Criteria
- Baseline runs are stable enough to detect regressions (document expected variance + thresholds).
- Harness can be driven by deterministic traces (record/replay) so profiles are comparable.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:35.347188171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:15.726253031Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:35.347188171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:35.347188171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:35.347188171Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.11","title":"Optional: Canvas2D fallback renderer","description":"Goal
- Provide a fallback renderer for environments without WebGPU.

Acceptance Criteria
- Same correctness semantics; perf can be lower but still usable for demos.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:39.725028591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:39.725028591Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:39.725028591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.11","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:39.725028591Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","title":"Optimize: WebGPU renderer hotspot matrix + one-lever passes","description":"Goal
- Apply the Extreme Optimization workflow to the web renderer.

Process
- Profile (CPU + GPU) with the harness.
- Build an opportunity matrix; implement only Score >= 2.0.
- For each change: isomorphism proof + golden checks.

Acceptance Criteria
- Documented perf wins with before/after p95 frame times.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:44.936674534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:09:08.120218876Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:44.936674534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:44.936674534Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:09:08.120172870Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:09:07.512839977Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.12","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:09:07.819055951Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+[package]
+name = "frankenterm-core"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
